summary: Removed the unused assembly file `asm/melee/cm/cmsnap.s`. The PR body states the file was not being linked, so keeping it in the tree only added stale assembly without affecting the build.

key_files.evidence: Only changed file; deleted 128 lines from an assembly source that the PR body says was not being linked.

what_changed: Removed unlinked assembly definitions for `func_800315C8`, `func_80031618`, `func_80031640`, and `func_800316B4`.
Removed associated local data/bss labels from the file, including `lbl_803BCD90`, `lbl_803BCD9C`, and `lbl_80453060`.
Removed string literals referencing `cmsnap.c` and the assertion text `_p(status) == CmSnapStatus_Sleep` from this unused assembly file.
smart_moves: Recognized that an assembly file can be safely removed when it is not linked, avoiding misleading stale source in the decomp tree.
Kept the change minimal: one file deleted, no unrelated edits.
Used deletion rather than attempting to decompile or maintain an unused object, reducing future confusion around nonexistent build coverage.
decomp_lessons: Before spending decompilation effort on an assembly file, verify that it is actually linked into the build.
Unlinked asm files may contain plausible function bodies and labels but still be irrelevant to matching progress.
Dead assembly sources should be removed or clearly excluded so future contributors do not treat them as active matching targets.
Build-system linkage is part of source-truth validation in a decomp project; files present under `asm/` are not necessarily active.

naming_conventions.note: File name suggests a `cm`/camera snapshot component, but this PR provides no confirmed C symbol names beyond strings and autogenerated labels.
The deleted file used address-based placeholder function and label names, indicating it had not been meaningfully symbolized.
Appears only inside a deleted assertion string; evidence is insufficient to claim a live enum or symbol definition.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: Do not assume every checked-in `.s` file participates in the link; confirm through linker scripts/build configuration first.
Removing unlinked assembly can be a valid cleanup tactic because it cannot affect matching output if it was never part of the linked binary.
Address-based labels and placeholder names in an unlinked file should not be propagated into headers or C declarations without additional evidence.

review_feedback.text: No review comments were present in the provided context. The PR had one review and was merged.

follow_up_queries: Check linker scripts or build manifests around PR #702 to confirm why `asm/melee/cm/cmsnap.s` was unlinked.
Search later PRs for `cmsnap`, `CmSnapStatus_Sleep`, or `func_800316B4` to see whether the functionality was reintroduced elsewhere.
Audit other assembly files under `asm/melee/cm/` for unlinked stale sources before decompiling them.
